Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a person in my situation, I've learned that when it comes to sensitive topics like healthcare and COVID vaccines, being straightforward but respectful is the way to go. You have to listen carefully first because everyone has different experiences or concerns. In my case, as someone who works in retail every day, I see how important it is for people to stay safe at work. Now with social media bringing up so many opinions monthly on these things, some are based on facts and some aren't.

I believe in getting information from reliable places like doctors or official websites, not just random posts online that pop up every few weeks during the pandemic discussions. Since I'm an immigrant living here now without family nearby except my partner, understanding proper healthcare can make a big difference. We need to be agreeable but firm when sharing our views—high agreeableness helps with this.

So in managing such conversations, start by asking questions politely: 'Could you tell me more about your thoughts?' Then share what I know from education level sources or personal experience working full-time these days.

My lower secondary schooling didn't cover much on vaccines specifically but gave good general knowledge. Still today's world with social media and all those hobbies doesn't change how we should approach serious matters like COVID protocols.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
1) Begin by actively listening to understand different perspectives without jumping into arguments.
2) Draw upon personal experience as a retail worker who values safety at work.
3) Reference reliable sources such as official health websites or medical professionals due to moderate neuroticism ensuring careful consideration of facts.
4) Incorporate background from being an immigrant with parents holding primary and vocational diplomas, highlighting the importance of accurate information for those adapting.
response_parsed.tone: Pragmatic yet empathetic, grounded in everyday experience
